title: Generalist medical practitioners
description: Administer anesthetics and analgesics for pain management prior to, during, or after surgery. Diagnose, treat, manage, and prevent diseases or conditions of the cardiovascular system. May further subspecialize in interventional procedures (e.g., balloon angioplasty and stent placement), echocardiography, or electrophysiology. Diagnose and treat diseases relating to the skin, hair, and nails. May perform both medical and dermatological surgery functions. (Based on 23 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prescribe or administer medication, therapy, treatment, vaccination, and other specialized medical care to treat or prevent illness, disease, or injury. Refer patients to medical specialists or other practitioners when necessary.
Direct and coordinate activities of nurses, students, assistants, specialists, therapists, and other medical staff.
Advise patients, parents or guardians, and community members concerning diet, activity, hygiene, and disease prevention.
Explain procedures and discuss test results or prescribed treatments with patients and guardians.
Make diagnoses for patients experiencing multiple illnesses or when the diagnosis is unclear, and analyze records, reports, test results, or examination information to diagnose medical conditions.
Monitor patients' conditions and progress, reevaluating treatments as necessary.
Manage and treat common health problems, serious, chronic, and complex illnesses in adolescents, adults, and the elderly, including providing long-term, comprehensive medical care.
Collect, record, and maintain patient information, including medical history and examination results.
Provide consulting services to other physicians and healthcare professionals regarding patient care and treatment.
Participate in continuing education activities and quality improvement initiatives to maintain knowledge and skills.
Provide information about potential health hazards and interventions to the public and professionals to improve community health.
Evaluate and manage chronic pain conditions, and develop treatment plans for patients, including addressing behavioral causes of diseases.
Administer anesthetics and analgesics for pain management prior to, during, or after surgery, and recognize or treat complications during procedures.
Coordinate work with other healthcare providers to ensure comprehensive patient care and implement health programs for prevention or treatment of injuries and illnesses.
Supervise or coordinate the work of healthcare staff to ensure effective patient care delivery.
Provide education and counseling on illness and injury prevention to patients and community members.
Operate on patients to remove, repair, or improve the functioning of diseased or injured body parts and systems. This includes performing surgeries for specific medical conditions.
Conduct research to develop or test medications, treatments, or procedures to prevent or control diseases or injuries, including conducting epidemiological investigations.
Develop and prescribe exercise programs and coordinate sports care activities with athletes and coaching staff.
Document or review comprehensive patient histories with attention to occupational or environmental risks.

Analyze patients' medical history, physical conditions, and examination results to verify the necessity of operations and determine the best procedures. Consult with other healthcare professionals to create treatment plans and recommendations for surgical procedures.
Refer patients to medical specialists or other practitioners when necessary and provide consultation and surgical assistance to physicians and surgeons.
Prepare and maintain case histories and patient records, including medical histories and examination results.

Monitor patients' recovery, making follow-up visits and using postoperative assessment techniques including blood and imaging tests. Monitor patients' conditions, reevaluate treatments as necessary, and provide inpatient or outpatient medical management of various conditions.

Perform surgical procedures, including transplantation operations, and operate on patients to correct deformities, repair injuries, prevent and treat diseases, and provide medical and dermatological surgeries as needed. Follow established surgical techniques during operations and preserve patients' health during surgical and obstetrical procedures.

Provide anesthetic or sedation during medical procedures and manage anesthesiological services, including coordinating them with other medical activities. Position patients for maximum comfort and accessibility during surgery and monitor them before, during, and after anesthesia.

Discuss surgical procedures, risks, and treatment results with patients and inform parents or guardians of health issues and surgical procedures through various communication channels. Explain procedures and discuss test results or prescribed treatments with patients.
Educate patients on management of their health and preventive measures while providing routine check-ups and assessing pain levels or treatment requirements.
Direct and coordinate the activities of medical staff, including nurses, specialists, and residents, to ensure quality patient care.

Conduct physical examinations and diagnostic tests on patients to provide information on their medical condition. Use records, reports, and examination data to diagnose medical conditions, including those related to the skin, hair, and nails, as well as cardiovascular health.
Perform necessary tests or procedures, such as laboratory tests and imaging, to diagnose conditions and decide on treatment plans for patients.
Conduct research on medical techniques or patient management strategies to improve procedures and outcomes in patient care or treatment of diseases.
Train or supervise medical students, residents, or other health professionals on relevant skills and knowledge in practice.
Engage in self-directed learning and participate in continuing education activities to enhance knowledge and skills in medical practices.
Perform autopsies to determine causes of deaths and review cases by analyzing autopsies, laboratory findings, or case investigation reports.

Conduct research and present scientific findings; participate in neuroscience research activities; conduct clinical or laboratory-based research in ophthalmology; and develop or implement plans and procedures for ophthalmologic services.
Educate physicians, students, and other personnel in medical laboratory professions, and provide training to medical students or staff members.

Develop or adopt new tests or instruments to improve diagnosis of diseases; read current literature, talk with colleagues, or participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in pathology; and monitor effectiveness of pain management interventions.

Diagnose or treat injuries, disorders, or diseases of the eye and eye structures, including performing comprehensive examinations of the visual system to determine the nature or extent of ocular disorders, prescribing ophthalmologic treatments, performing ophthalmic surgeries, and documenting examination results, treatment plans, and patients' outcomes.

Perform, order, or interpret the results of diagnostic or clinical tests, including those related to the ocular system, and analyze and interpret results from tests such as microbial or parasite tests, urine analyses, hormonal assays, and other diagnostic procedures.

Consult or coordinate with other rehabilitative professionals, including physical and occupational therapists, rehabilitation nurses, speech pathologists, neuropsychologists, behavioral psychologists, social workers, or medical technicians, and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams of health professionals to provide optimal patient care.

Prescribe physical therapy to relax the muscles and improve strength, develop comprehensive plans for immediate and long-term rehabilitation including therapeutic exercise, and assess characteristics of patients' pain and document them accordingly.
Examine patients to assess mobility, strength, communication, or cognition, and monitor effectiveness of pain management interventions, such as medication or spinal injections.
Admit patients for hospital stays, diagnose, treat or provide continuous care to hospital inpatients, and conduct discharge planning and discharge patients.
Communicate pathologic findings to surgeons or other physicians and coordinate neurological services with other health care team activities.
Prescribe medications or treatment regimens to hospital inpatients, and prescribe or administer medications, such as anti-epileptic drugs, determining the risks and benefits.

Refer patients to other health care practitioners as necessary, and identify and treat major neurological system diseases and disorders, such as central nervous system infection, cranio spinal trauma, dementia, and stroke.

Inform patients or families of neurological diagnoses and prognoses, or benefits, risks, and costs of various treatment plans and develop treatment plans based on diagnoses and on evaluation of factors, such as age and general health, or procedural risks and costs.

Document or evaluate patients' medical histories; communicate with other health care professionals regarding patients' conditions and care; and interview patients to obtain information about complaints, symptoms, medical histories, and family histories.

Diagnose diseases or study medical conditions, using techniques such as gross pathology, histology, cytology, clinical chemistry, immunology, and molecular biology, also involving examining microscopic samples to identify diseases or other abnormalities.
Analyze and interpret the results from tests to make informed decisions about patient care, including laboratory analyses of patients' blood or cerebrospinal fluid.
Participate in continuing education activities to maintain and expand competence, and engage in quality improvement projects or safety programs.
Testify in depositions or trials as an expert witness, and participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in practice.
